item.title: Photos: Reclaimed by Nature
item.description: On Earth Day, I wanted to share images of the incredible resilience of nature: the many ways that plants, animals, and natural processes reclaim abandoned human places and find ways to thrive.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Hegseth to the Troops: We Are Bringing Back the Flu!
item.description: Grab your cough drops. We’re going to battle.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri

item.title: Trump’s Enormous Gerrymandering Blunder
item.description: The Republican redistricting effort backfires.
item.dc:creator: Russell Berman

item.title: Eight of the Most Fascinating Biographies to Read
item.description: Each is animated by the author’s love—for their subject, for language, and for pushing the boundaries of what the genre can do.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Boggs

item.title: Apple Is Boring Now
item.description: Fifteen years after Steve Jobs’s death, the company is a successful, if unexciting, powerhouse.
item.dc:creator: Ian Bogost

item.title: Brace for the Plastic Price Hikes
item.description: The war in Iran could raise the consumer costs of car parts, toys, clothing, and more.
item.dc:creator: Beth Gardiner

item.title: The Iran Talks Are Making India Feel Small
item.description: Modi styled himself a global leader but can’t seem to get ahead of events in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Vaibhav Vats

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Black Comedy
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Is Cohabitation the Feminist Future?
item.description: Stories about women living together are proliferating—and offering alternative visions to the nuclear family.
item.dc:creator: Lily Meyer

item.title: What Abdul El-Sayed Doesn’t Get About Trump
item.description: How Democrats can lose Michigan, again
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: A ‘Barbaric’ Problem in American Hospitals Is Only Getting Bigger
item.description: Patients are getting stuck in the emergency department for days while waiting for a spot in an inpatient ward.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Rosenthal

item.title: The ‘Missing-Scientist’ Story Is Unbelievably Dumb
item.description: It is, in a way, a remarkable achievement.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Engber

item.title: Another Trump Cabinet Member Departs in Scandal
item.description: Lori Chavez-DeRemer was supposed to take the GOP in a more worker-friendly direction. Instead she joins a group of dismissed administration officials.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: A Life Hack for the Ultra-Wealthy Is Going Mainstream
item.description: More families who can afford it are hiring a house manager, a kind of “chief of staff for the home.”
item.dc:creator: Nancy Walecki

item.title: Elon Musk’s SpaceX Endgame
item.description: The world’s richest man is accruing more power than ever before.
item.dc:creator: Quinn Slobodian

item.title: DefineJewish State
item.description: The phrase is even more complicated than it appears.
item.dc:creator: Adam Serwer

item.title: Big Sky Crack-Up
item.description: Montana Democrats thought they found a novel way to win control of the U.S. Senate—until the party faithful started fighting back.
item.dc:creator: Michael Scherer

item.title: The Rise of CliffsNotes Cinema
item.description: Oversimplified literary remakes miss the point of the works they are adapting.
item.dc:creator: Shirley Li

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Deadly Sins
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Why Justin Bieber Played YouTube Onstage for Thousands of People
item.description: The pop star transformed the normal act of browsing your laptop into something interesting—and unsettling.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kornhaber
